title: Liput liehumassa
summaries: "Liput liehumassa" by Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson is a novel written in the early 20th century. As the story unfolds, we learn of various characters, including Konrad Kurt, known for his struggles with alcohol and familial connections, which significantly shape his identity and actions. The opening establishes a rich tapestry of life surrounding the estate, hinting at deeper emotional conflicts and societal issues that are likely to unfold as the story progresses.
